DECLARATION OF SUSANNA CHENETTE IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER AND DISCOVERY STAY AND REFERENCE TO EARLY SETTLEMENT PROGRAM (TRANSACTION ID # 61744671) FILED BY DEFENDANT 235 BERRY RESIDENTIAL ASSOCIATION A CALIFORNIA NONPROFIT MUTUAL BENEFIT CORPORATION

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT 235 BERRY RESIDENTIAL ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER AND DISCOVERY STAY AND REFERENCE TO EARLY SETTLEMENT PROGRAM IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE DEPOSITION OF SUSANNA CHENETTE SHALL BE TAKEN IN SAN FRANCISCO AT A FACILITY ADEQUATE FOR THE PROXIMITY AND CARE OF HER MINOR CHILDREN BY A CAREGIVER TO BE PROVIDED BY MS. CHENETTE. THE MOTION IS OTHERWISE DENIED FOR LACK OF AN ADEQUATE SHOWING OF NONAVAILABILITY AS TO DEAN GRIFFITHS, GROUNDS FOR A FURTHER PROTECTIVE ORDER OR DISCOVERY STAY OR AUTHORITY FOR A REFERENCE TO THE EARLY SETTLEMENT PROGRAM. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES OR ORDER FOR THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE PRO TEM: JAMES H. FLEMING, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, MATTER NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-18-2018 Vacated

Notice Of Motion For Protective Order And Discovery Stay And Reference To Early Settlement Program
Off Calendar Mar-22-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

There being no appearances, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on Defendant 235 Berry Residential Association's Motion For Protective Order And Discovery Stay And Reference To Early Settlement Program as follows: The motion for protective order, discovery stay and reference to early settlement program is granted in part and denied in part as follows: the deposition of Susanna Chenette shall be taken in San Francisco at a facility adequate for the proximity and care of her minor children by a caregiver to be provided by Ms. Chenette. Further, adequate breaks shall be taken to allow breastfeeding of Ms. Chenette's infant child. The motion is otherwise denied for lack of an adequate showing of nonavailability as to Dean Griffiths, grounds for a further protective order or discovery stay or authority for a reference to the early settlement program. The parties are reminded of the availability of the mandatory fee arbitration program administered by the State Bar and the Bar Association of San Francisco (which includes mediation) and the arbitration clause of the parties' fee agreement, resort to which would result in a stay of this action. Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: James H. Fleming, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. =(302/JPT/HEK))

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-28-2018 Set For Trial On Oct-09-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
